name: Lemon
image_alt: Round yellow cucumber fruit resembling a lemon
description: A round, pale yellow cucumber about the size and shape of a lemon. Thin skin, crisp white flesh, and an exceptionally mild, never-bitter flavor. One of the most widely loved specialty cucumbers.
history: An American heirloom documented since the 1890s. The pale color and round shape were initially a novelty, but the reliable mild flavor and thin skin won it a permanent place in American seed catalogs.

good.reason: Attracts pollinators critical for cucumber fruiting

seasonal_quirks: Skin toughens and flavor can become slightly bitter if left on vine too long — harvest promptly at lemon-yellow color. Seeds are small and tender when picked at the right time.
harvest_cues: Harvest when fully lemon-yellow but before white streaking appears. Firm to the touch. Size of a large lemon or baseball — 2.5–3 inches across.
variety_notes: The thin skin means these don't need peeling. Seeds are usually tender enough to eat at proper harvest stage. Extremely prolific — one or two plants is usually enough.
preservation: Excellent quick-pickled in rice vinegar with dill. Refrigerator pickles.
